title: Some users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service
impact: Users' Microsoft Intune devices may have been unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service.

updates.content:
Title: Some users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service

User impact: Users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service.

More info: Additionally, some admins may be unable to load the Intune Admin console 'Scripts and Remediations' blade and get a "Request policy is null" error.

Current status: We're investigating a potential issue with Microsoft Intune and checking for impact to your organization. We'll provide an update within 30 minutes.

Title: Some users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service

User impact: Users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service.

More info: Additionally, some admins may be unable to load the Intune Admin console 'Scripts and Remediations' blade and get a "Request policy is null" error.

Current status: After our analysis of service health telemetry, we suspect that an authentication component has been misconfigured, and we're confirming this is the source of the impact in order to develop a solution to resolve the issue.

Scope of impact: Some users and admins in North America, Europe, Middle East, and Africa attempting to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service on Microsoft Intune devices or load the Intune Admin console 'Scripts and Remediations' blade may be impacted.

Next update by: Thursday, May 21, 2026, at 5:30 PM UTC

Title: Some users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service

User impact: Users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service.

More info: Additionally, some admins may be unable to load the Intune Admin console 'Scripts and Remediations' blade and get a "Request policy is null" error.

Current status: We've confirmed a misconfigured authentication component is resulting in the impact, and we're manually correcting the misconfiguration to remediate the impact.

Scope of impact: Some users and admins in North America, Europe, Middle East, and Africa attempting to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service on Microsoft Intune devices or load the Intune Admin console 'Scripts and Remediations' blade may be impacted.

Root cause: A misconfigured authentication component is resulting in the impact.

Next update by: Thursday, May 21, 2026, at 7:30 PM UTC

Title: Some users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service

User impact: Users' Microsoft Intune devices may have been unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service.

More info: Additionally, some admins may have been unable to load the Intune Admin console 'Scripts and Remediations' blade and got a "Request policy is null" error.

Final status: We've completed the reconfiguration of the authentication component and confirmed after monitoring service health telemetry that the associated failures are no longer occurring and the impact is remediated.

Scope of impact: Some users and admins in North America, Europe, Middle East, and Africa who attempted to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service on Microsoft Intune devices or load the Intune Admin console 'Scripts and Remediations' blade may have been impacted.

Start time: Wednesday, May 20, 2026, at 12:29 AM UTC

End time: Thursday, May 21, 2026, at 6:20 PM UTC

Root cause: A misconfigured authentication component resulted in the impact.

Next steps:  
- We're continuing to analyze the impacted component to understand how it became misconfigured and take any necessary actions to ensure the component remains configured as expected to prevent this issue from reoccurring.

This is the final update for the event.
